title: Neuma
description: NeuMa is a multimodal neuromarketing dataset comprising simultaneous EEG, eye tracking, and behavioral recordings from 42 participants during an advertising brochure-browsing task. Participants viewed supermarket brochures and selected products they intended to purchase while their neural activity, gaze patterns, mouse interactions, and demographic/product preferences were recorded. This dataset integrates neurophysiological and behavioral measures to investigate consumer decision-making processes.
methods_description: Data collection included concurrent EEG recordings, eye tracking, questionnaire responses (demographic, profiling, and product-related), and computer mouse tracking during a supermarket brochure-browsing scenario where participants selected products for purchase.
